seoTitle: How to Link Salesforce With Outlook (Integration Guide)
metaDescription: Connect Salesforce to Outlook with the official add-in: authenticate your CRM, pin key fields to the side panel, and log emails to opportunities.
intro: Manually copying client correspondence between your inbox and CRM wastes time and creates room for mistakes. This guide covers installing the official Salesforce add-in for Outlook, authenticating your account, customizing your side panel, and logging emails directly to CRM records.

steps.heading: Open the Microsoft AppSource store
steps.body: From within Outlook, access the AppSource marketplace to search for integrations.

steps.heading: Install the Salesforce add-in
steps.body: Search for the official Salesforce add-in and click Add to install it.

steps.heading: Authenticate your account
steps.body: Launch the Salesforce side panel, choose Production or Sandbox, and log in with your CRM credentials.

steps.heading: Customize your side panel
steps.body: Configure the panel to pin high-value fields like Leads, Contacts, and Cases for quick reference.

steps.heading: Log emails to CRM records
steps.body: Use the one-click log email feature to attach important client threads to active opportunity records.

steps.heading: Test the sync
steps.body: Run a live test to confirm updates display correctly across both Outlook and Salesforce.

faq.question: Do I need admin permissions to install the Salesforce add-in?
faq.answer: Installing add-ins from AppSource may require admin approval depending on your organization's Microsoft 365 policies.

faq.question: Can I connect to a Salesforce Sandbox for testing?
faq.answer: Yes, the add-in lets you choose between logging into Production or Sandbox environments during setup.

faq.question: Does logging an email to Salesforce also sync attachments?
faq.answer: The log email feature is designed to attach the message thread to the relevant record, though attachment handling can depend on your configuration.

faq.question: Will my calendar meetings sync too, not just emails?
faq.answer: The integration is primarily built around email logging and CRM record visibility rather than full calendar synchronization.
